Tests duplicate label error handling in JavaScript labelled statements.

# Purpose
This code is a simple JavaScript script that demonstrates the use of labeled statements and the `break` statement. It defines a label `a` and uses it to label a `for` loop that iterates from 0 to 9. The `break a;` statement immediately exits the loop on its first iteration, effectively preventing the loop from executing more than once. This example illustrates how labels can be used to control the flow of loops in JavaScript.
